title: Fold-Safe Categorical and Binned Features
summary: Treat the small categorical columns and coarse numeric bins as first-class signals instead of leaving them as raw text or continuous values only.
feature_strategy: One-hot encode spectral_type and galaxy_population, add fold-safe frequency/likelihood encodings for those categories, bin redshift and selected magnitudes into quantiles, and create small cross features such as spectral_type x redshift_bin and galaxy_population x color_bin; keep all encodings out-of-fold to avoid leakage.
baseline_model_panel: Balanced logistic regression, categorical-boosting style trees, and a class-weighted gradient-boosted tree model.
model_panel_rationale: These features are deliberately low-dimensional and tabular, so a linear baseline checks whether the bins/encodings already separate classes while tree models test whether the categorical interactions matter.
validation_strategy: 5-fold StratifiedKFold with all frequency/target-like encodings computed strictly on the training folds only.
materialization_hint: Materialize the categorical block separately from the numeric block, compute bin boundaries and any count-based transforms inside each fold, and keep the set of interactions small so the experiment isolates whether coarse segmentation helps the classifier.
expected_signal: If useful, the fold-local encodings should lift validation without large runtime cost, especially on the minority class recall that balanced accuracy rewards; gains should be visible even before complex models are introduced.
risk: Any leakage in the encodings will inflate CV, and overly fine bins may fragment the data enough to hurt balanced accuracy more than they help.
